nodes.label: Unit 3: Run! / Nasruddin's Aim
nodes.description: Poem about running, a humorous Nasruddin story, and grammar on verbs

nodes.label: Run! (Poem)
nodes.description: A lively poem celebrating running, outdoor play, and exercise

nodes.label: Theme: Joy of Movement
nodes.description: Running outdoors makes us happy, healthy, and full of energy

nodes.label: Rhyme & Rhythm
nodes.description: Short lines, rhyming words like run/sun, fun/done make it musical

nodes.label: Mood: Happy & Energetic
nodes.description: The poem creates a cheerful, active feeling that makes you want to play

nodes.label: Nasruddin's Aim (Story)
nodes.description: A humorous folk tale about Nasruddin boasting about his archery skills

nodes.label: Mulla Nasruddin
nodes.description: A famous folk tale character known for his witty and funny stories

nodes.label: Plot: Boasting & Missing
nodes.description: Nasruddin boasts about his aim, misses the target, then makes clever excuses

nodes.label: Moral: Don't Boast
nodes.description: Do not brag about your abilities; accept mistakes honestly

nodes.label: Grammar: Verbs
nodes.description: Action words that tell us what someone or something does

nodes.label: What are Verbs?
nodes.description: Words that show actions: run, jump, eat, sleep, write, play

nodes.label: Verb Forms
nodes.description: run/runs/running - verbs change form based on who does the action and when

nodes.label: Verbs in Sentences
nodes.description: Every sentence needs a verb: 'She runs fast.' 'He shot the arrow.'

nodes.label: Verbs from the Chapter
nodes.description: run, jump, shoot, miss, hit, aim, boast, laugh - all action words from Unit 3
